content.thinking:
Good, I now understand the conventions:
- Imports use `@/` path aliases
- API routes use `NextRequest` from `next/server`, and `Response.json()` for responses
- Error handling pattern with AuthError
- The database uses Prisma with PrismaPg

Now let me check the Prisma schema to understand the data models (AssetModel, AssetInstance, Ticket, etc.)

2	  provider = "prisma-client-js"
3	}
4

7	}
8

58	  PENDING_IT

60	  REJECTED

81	model Department {
82	  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
83	  name      String
84	  managerId String?  @unique @map("manager_id")
85	
86	  manager           User?           @relation("DepartmentManager", fields: [managerId], references: [id])
87	  users             User[]

90	
91	  @@map("departments")

93	
94	model User {
95	  id           String     @id @default(cuid())
96	  email        String     @unique
97	  passwordHash String     @map("password_hash")
98	  name         String
99	  role         UserRole
100	  deptId       String     @map("dept_id")
101	  status       UserStatus @default(ACTIVE)
102	
103	  department        Department          @relation(fields: [deptId], references: [id])
104	  managedDept       Department?         @relation("DepartmentManager")
105	  heldAssets        AssetInstance[]
106	  appliedTickets    Ticket[]            @relation("Applicant")

109	  deputyApprovals   Ticket[]            @relation("DeputyApprover")
110	  fromHolderTickets Ticket[]            @relation("FromHolder")
111	  toHolderTickets   Ticket[]            @relation("ToHolder")
112	  auditLogs         AuditLog[]
113	  notifications     Notification[]
114	  inventorySessions InventorySession[]
115	
116	  @@map("users")
117	}
118	
119	model AssetModel {
120	  id             String             @id
121	  category       AssetModelCategory
122	  brandName      String             @map("brand_name")
123	  modelName      String             @map("model_name")

125	  purchasePrice  Decimal            @map("purchase_price") @db.Decimal(10, 2)
126	  supplier       String

129	
130	  instances     AssetInstance[]

133	  @@map("asset_models")
134	}
135	
136	model AssetInstance {
137	  id             String               @id
138	  sn             String               @unique
139	  modelId        String               @map("model_id")
140	  status         AssetInstanceStatus
141	  holderId       String?              @map("holder_id")
142	  deptId         String?              @map("dept_id")
143	  purchaseDate   DateTime             @map("purchase_date")
144	  warrantyExpiry DateTime             @map("warranty_expiry")
145	  scrapReason    String?              @map("scrap_reason") @db.Text
146	  lostReason     String?              @map("lost_reason") @db.Text
147	  createdAt      DateTime             @default(now()) @map("created_at")
148	  updatedAt      DateTime             @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
149	
150	  model       AssetModel          @relation(fields: [modelId], references: [id])
151	  holder      User?               @relation(fields: [holderId], references: [id])
152	  department  Department?         @relation(fields: [deptId], references: [id])

168	  rejectReason    String?      @map("reject_reason") @db.Text
169	  aiTriageResult  Json?        @map("ai_triage_result")

171	  forceRepair     Boolean      @default(false) @map("force_repair")
172	  assignedById    String?      @map("assigned_by_id")
173	  processorId     String?      @map("processor_id")
174	  fromHolderId    String?      @map("from_holder_id")
175	  toHolderId      String?      @map("to_holder_id")

177	  deputyApproval  Boolean      @default(false) @map("deputy_approval")
178	  deputyApproverId String?     @map("deputy_approver_id")
179	  completedAt     DateTime?    @map("completed_at")
180	  createdAt       DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")
181	  updatedAt       DateTime     @updatedAt @map("updated_at")
182	
183	  applicant      User           @relation("Applicant", fields: [applicantId], references: [id])
184	  targetAsset    AssetInstance? @relation(fields: [targetAssetId], references: [id])
185	  targetModel    AssetModel?    @relation(fields: [targetModelId], references: [id])
186	  assignedBy     User?          @relation("AssignedBy", fields: [assignedById], references: [id])
187	  processor      User?          @relation("Processor", fields: [processorId], references: [id])
188	  fromHolder     User?          @relation("FromHolder", fields: [fromHolderId], references: [id])
189	  toHolder       User?          @relation("ToHolder", fields: [toHolderId], references: [id])
190	  targetDept     Department?    @relation(fields: [targetDeptId], references: [id])
191	  deputyApprover User?          @relation("DeputyApprover", fields: [deputyApproverId], references: [id])
192	
193	  notifications Notification[]
194	
195	  @@map("tickets")

197

204	  beforeSnapshot Json?    @map("before_snapshot")

211	  @@map("audit_logs")

213

223	  createdAt      DateTime         @default(now()) @map("created_at")

225	  user         User            @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
226	  relatedTicket Ticket?         @relation(fields: [relatedTicketId], references: [id])
227	  relatedAsset AssetInstance?   @relation(fields: [relatedAssetId], references: [id])
228	
229	  @@map("notifications")

231

234	  startedById String                   @map("started_by_id")

237	  completedAt DateTime?                @map("completed_at")

240	  scans     InventoryScan[]

242	  @@map("inventory_sessions")

244	
245	model InventoryScan {
246	  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
247	  sessionId String   @map("session_id")

249	  scannedAt DateTime @default(now()) @map("scanned_at")
250	
251	  session InventorySession @relation(fields: [sessionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
252	  asset   AssetInstance    @relation(fields: [assetId], references: [id])
253	
254	  @@unique([sessionId, assetId])
255	  @@map("inventory_scans")

content.thinking: Let me read NextRequest and NextResponse docs to understand the latest API.

content.content:
1	---
2	title: NextResponse
3	description: API Reference for NextResponse.
4	---
5	
6	{/* The content of this doc is shared between the app and pages router. You can use the `<PagesOnly>Content</PagesOnly>` component to add content that is specific to the Pages Router. Any shared content should not be wrapped in a component. */}
7	
8	NextResponse extends the [Web Response API](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Response) with additional convenience methods.
9	
10	## `cookies`
11	
12	Read or mutate the [`Set-Cookie`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Set-Cookie) header of the response.
13	
14	### `set(name, value)`
15	
16	Given a name, set a cookie with the given value on the response.
17	
18	```ts
19	// Given incoming request /home
20	let response = NextResponse.next()
21	// Set a cookie to hide the banner
22	response.cookies.set('show-banner', 'false')
23	// Response will have a `Set-Cookie:show-banner=false;path=/home` header
24	return response
25	```
26	
27	### `get(name)`
28	
29	Given a cookie name, return the value of the cookie. If the cookie is not found, `undefined` is returned. If multiple cookies are found, the first one is returned.
30	
31	```ts
32	// Given incoming request /home
33	let response = NextResponse.next()
34	// { name: 'show-banner', value: 'false', Path: '/home' }
35	response.cookies.get('show-banner')
36	```
37	
38	### `getAll()`
39	
40	Given a cookie name, return the values of the cookie. If no name is given, return all cookies on the response.
41	
42	```ts
43	// Given incoming request /home
44	let response = NextResponse.next()
45	// [
46	//   { name: 'experiments', value: 'new-pricing-page', Path: '/home' },
47	//   { name: 'experiments', value: 'winter-launch', Path: '/home' },
48	// ]
49	response.cookies.getAll('experiments')
50	// Alternatively, get all cookies for the response
51	response.cookies.getAll()
52	```
53	
54	### `has(name)`
55	
56	Given a cookie name, return `true` if the cookie exists on the response.
57	
58	```ts
59	// Given incoming request /home
60	let response = NextResponse.next()
61	// Returns true if cookie exists, false if it does not
62	response.cookies.has('experiments')
63	```
64	
65	### `delete(name)`
66	
67	Given a cookie name, delete the cookie from the response.
68	
69	```ts
70	// Given incoming request /home
71	let response = NextResponse.next()
72	// Returns true for deleted, false if nothing is deleted
73	response.cookies.delete('experiments')
74	```
75	
76	## `json()`
77	
78	Produce a response with the given JSON body.
79	
80	```ts filename="app/api/route.ts" switcher
81	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
82	
83	export async function GET(request: Request) {
84	  return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Internal Server Error' }, { status: 500 })
85	}
86	```
87	
88	```js filename="app/api/route.js" switcher
89	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
90	
91	export async function GET(request) {
92	  return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Internal Server Error' }, { status: 500 })
93	}
94	```
95	
96	## `redirect()`
97	
98	Produce a response that redirects to a [URL](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/URL).
99	
100	```ts
101	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
102	
103	return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/new', request.url))
104	```
105	
106	The [URL](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/URL) can be created and modified before being used in the `NextResponse.redirect()` method. For example, you can use the `request.nextUrl` property to get the current URL, and then modify it to redirect to a different URL.
107	
108	```ts
109	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
110	
111	// Given an incoming request...
112	const loginUrl = new URL('/login', request.url)
113	// Add ?from=/incoming-url to the /login URL
114	loginUrl.searchParams.set('from', request.nextUrl.pathname)
115	// And redirect to the new URL
116	return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl)
117	```
118	
119	## `rewrite()`
120	
121	Produce a response that rewrites (proxies) the given [URL](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/URL) while preserving the original URL.
122	
123	```ts
124	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
125	
126	// Incoming request: /about, browser shows /about
127	// Rewritten request: /proxy, browser shows /about
128	return NextResponse.rewrite(new URL('/proxy', request.url))
129	```
130	
131	## `next()`
132	
133	The `next()` method is useful for Proxy, as it allows you to return early and continue routing.
134	
135	```ts
136	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
137	
138	return NextResponse.next()
139	```
140	
141	You can also forward `headers` upstream when producing the response, using `NextResponse.next({ request: { headers } })`:
142	
143	```ts
144	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server'
145	
146	// Given an incoming request...
147	const newHeaders = new Headers(request.headers)
148	// Add a new header
149	newHeaders.set('x-version', '123')
150	// Forward the modified request headers upstream
151	return NextResponse.next({
152	  request: {
153	    // New request headers
154	    headers: newHeaders,
155	  },
156	})
157	```
158	
159	This forwards `newHeaders` upstream to the target page, route, or server action, and does not expose them to the client. While this pattern is useful for passing data upstream, it should be used with caution because the headers containing this data may be forwarded to external services.
160	
161	In contrast, `NextResponse.next({ headers })` is a shorthand for sending headers from proxy to the client. This is **NOT** good practice and should be avoided. Among other reasons because setting response headers like `Content-Type`, can override framework expectations (for example, the `Content-Type` used by Server Actions), leading to failed submissions or broken streaming responses.
162	
163	```ts
164	import { type NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
165	
166	async function proxy(request: NextRequest) {
167	  const headers = await injectAuth(request.headers)
168	  // DO NOT forward headers like this
169	  return NextResponse.next({ headers })
170	}
171	```
172	
173	In general, avoid copying all incoming request headers because doing so can leak sensitive data to clients or upstream services.
174	
175	Prefer a defensive approach by creating a subset of incoming request headers using an allow-list. For example, you might discard custom `x-*` headers and only forward known-safe headers:
176	
177	```ts
178	import { type NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
179	
180	function proxy(request: NextRequest) {
181	  const incoming = new Headers(request.headers)
182	  const forwarded = new Headers()
183	
184	  for (const [name, value] of incoming) {
185	    const headerName = name.toLowerCase()
186	    // Keep only known-safe headers, discard custom x-* and other sensitive ones
187	    if (
188	      !headerName.startsWith('x-') &&
189	      headerName !== 'authorization' &&
190	      headerName !== 'cookie'
191	    ) {
192	      // Preserve original header name casing
193	      forwarded.set(name, value)
194	    }
195	  }
196	
197	  return NextResponse.next({
198	    request: {
199	      headers: forwarded,
200	    },
201	  })
202	}
203	```
204

1	---
2	title: NextRequest
3	description: API Reference for NextRequest.
4	---
5	
6	{/* The content of this doc is shared between the app and pages router. You can use the `<PagesOnly>Content</PagesOnly>` component to add content that is specific to the Pages Router. Any shared content should not be wrapped in a component. */}
7	
8	NextRequest extends the [Web Request API](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/Request) with additional convenience methods.
9	
10	## `cookies`
11	
12	Read or mutate the [`Set-Cookie`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Set-Cookie) header of the request.
13	
14	### `set(name, value)`
15	
16	Given a name, set a cookie with the given value on the request.
17	
18	```ts
19	// Given incoming request /home
20	// Set a cookie to hide the banner
21	// request will have a `Set-Cookie:show-banner=false;path=/home` header
22	request.cookies.set('show-banner', 'false')
23	```
24	
25	### `get(name)`
26	
27	Given a cookie name, return the value of the cookie. If the cookie is not found, `undefined` is returned. If multiple cookies are found, the first one is returned.
28	
29	```ts
30	// Given incoming request /home
31	// { name: 'show-banner', value: 'false', Path: '/home' }
32	request.cookies.get('show-banner')
33	```
34	
35	### `getAll()`
36	
37	Given a cookie name, return the values of the cookie. If no name is given, return all cookies on the request.
38	
39	```ts
40	// Given incoming request /home
41	// [
42	//   { name: 'experiments', value: 'new-pricing-page', Path: '/home' },
43	//   { name: 'experiments', value: 'winter-launch', Path: '/home' },
44	// ]
45	request.cookies.getAll('experiments')
46	// Alternatively, get all cookies for the request
47	request.cookies.getAll()
48	```
49	
50	### `delete(name)`
51	
52	Given a cookie name, delete the cookie from the request.
53	
54	```ts
55	// Returns true for deleted, false is nothing is deleted
56	request.cookies.delete('experiments')
57	```
58	
59	### `has(name)`
60	
61	Given a cookie name, return `true` if the cookie exists on the request.
62	
63	```ts
64	// Returns true if cookie exists, false if it does not
65	request.cookies.has('experiments')
66	```
67	
68	### `clear()`
69	
70	Remove all cookies from the request.
71	
72	```ts
73	request.cookies.clear()
74	```
75	
76	## `nextUrl`
77	
78	Extends the native [`URL`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/URL) API with additional convenience methods, including Next.js specific properties.
79	
80	```ts
81	// Given a request to /home, pathname is /home
82	request.nextUrl.pathname
83	// Given a request to /home?name=lee, searchParams is { 'name': 'lee' }
84	request.nextUrl.searchParams
85	```
86	
87	The following options are available:
88	
89	<PagesOnly>
90	
91	| Property          | Type                      | Description                                                                                                                            |
92	| ----------------- | ------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
93	| `basePath`        | `string`                  | The [base path](/docs/pages/api-reference/config/next-config-js/basePath) of the URL.                                                  |
94	| `buildId`         | `string` \| `undefined`   | The build identifier of the Next.js application. Can be [customized](/docs/pages/api-reference/config/next-config-js/generateBuildId). |
95	| `defaultLocale`   | `string` \| `undefined`   | The default locale for [internationalization](/docs/pages/guides/internationalization).                                                |
96	| `domainLocale`    |                           |                                                                                                                                        |
97	| - `defaultLocale` | `string`                  | The default locale within a domain.                                                                                                    |
98	| - `domain`        | `string`                  | The domain associated with a specific locale.                                                                                          |
99	| - `http`          | `boolean` \| `undefined`  | Indicates if the domain is using HTTP.                                                                                                 |
100	| `locales`         | `string[]` \| `undefined` | An array of available locales.                                                                                                         |
101	| `locale`          | `string` \| `undefined`   | The currently active locale.                                                                                                           |
102	| `url`             | `URL`                     | The URL object.                                                                                                                        |
103	
104	</PagesOnly>
105	
106	<AppOnly>
107	
108	| Property       | Type                    | Description                                                                                                                          |
109	| -------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
110	| `basePath`     | `string`                | The [base path](/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/basePath) of the URL.                                                  |
111	| `buildId`      | `string` \| `undefined` | The build identifier of the Next.js application. Can be [customized](/docs/app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/generateBuildId). |
112	| `pathname`     | `string`                | The pathname of the URL.                                                                                                             |
113	| `searchParams` | `Object`                | The search parameters of the URL.                                                                                                    |
114	
115	> **Note:** The internationalization properties from the Pages Router are not available for usage in the App Router. Learn more about [internationalization with the App Router](/docs/app/guides/internationalization).
116	
117	</AppOnly>
118	
119	## Version History
120	
121	| Version   | Changes                 |
122	| --------- | ----------------------- |
123	| `v15.0.0` | `ip` and `geo` removed. |
124

content.text:
Phase 2 (AI 集成) 已完成，TypeScript 零错误。以下是创建的文件清单：

**`lib/ai/` 核心模块（4 个文件）：**

- `/Users/admin/it-asset/lib/ai/client.ts` — DeepSeek V4 Flash API 客户端，支持 AbortController 超时、JSON response_format、类型泛型
- `/Users/admin/it-asset/lib/ai/triage.ts` — 故障分诊规则强制函数（high severity -> needRepair=true, needRepair=true -> selfHelp=null, 等兜底逻辑）
- `/Users/admin/it-asset/lib/ai/confirmation-token.ts` — 基于 jose SignJWT HS256 的 5 分钟有效期确认令牌，附带一次性使用黑名单
- `/Users/admin/it-asset/lib/ai/with-fallback.ts` — 通用降级包装器，try/catch 后返回 `{ ok, data }` 或 `{ ok: false, fallback }` 格式

**`app/api/ai/` 路由（3 个文件）：**

- `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/ai/recommend/route.ts` — POST，EMPLOYEE/MANAGER 权限，查询 ACTIVE 型号库存后调用 DeepSeek 推荐，降级文案："推荐服务暂时不可用，请稍后重试或浏览全部可用型号自行选择。"
- `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/ai/triage/route.ts` — POST，EMPLOYEE/MANAGER 权限，AI 诊断后经 `enforceTriageRules` 后处理，降级文案："故障诊断暂时不可用，你的报修工单已提交，IT 管理员将尽快处理。"
- `/Users/admin/it-asset/app/api/ai/chat/route.ts` — POST，任何认证用户，查询用户持有的资产和待处理工单作为上下文，needConfirmation=true 时自动生成 confirmationToken，降级文案："AI 助手暂时不可用，你可以通过「我的资产」查看资产信息..."

所有端点均遵循项目既有的 `verifyAuth`/`AuthError` 错误处理模式和 `NextRequest` + `Response.json()` 规范。启动项目前需在 `.env` 中配置 `AI_API_KEY`。
